source_formulae: P0R02840: Quasicriticality is the optimal dynamic regime for performing inference. The entire SCPN architecture is an inference engine, and maintaining this state is essential for its function.

specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 3.2 The Dynamic Spine: The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > Quasicriticality and Self-Organisation > Meta-Framework Integrations > Predictive Coding Integration
source_formulae: P0R02842: A system at the "edge of chaos" is maximally sensitive to new information. Its state is flexible enough to be updated by even faint prediction error signals, but stable enough not to be completely disrupted by them. Quasicriticality, therefore, optimises the trade-off between the stability of the generative model and its ability to learn from new evidence.
test_protocols: preserve Maximising Inferential Capacity: source-accounting boundary

specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 3.2 The Dynamic Spine: The Unified Phase Dynamics Equation (UPDE) > Quasicriticality and Self-Organisation > Meta-Framework Integrations > Predictive Coding Integration
source_formulae: P0R02844: The mechanism of Self-Organised Criticality is a form of meta-inference; it is the process by which the system tunes its own precision. The branching parameter (sigmaL) is a measure of the system's confidence in its own dynamics. The homeostatic plasticity rules are the algorithm the system uses to ensure its own internal states are weighted with the optimal level of precision to facilitate efficient learning and prevent it from becoming either rigid (delusional) or chaotic (confused).
test_protocols: preserve SOC as Precision Tuning: source-accounting boundary
